recalls.Description:
The recall affects a set of 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms that are powered by a 9V battery. These alarms feature a light sound warning and a test button, and they are designed in a circular white shape. The model number "XG-7D04-KZ9Z" and SKU "CX-50YP-A5VN" can be found on the bottom of each unit. Consumers should be aware that if the alarm's sensing threshold is excessively high, it may not activate in time, creating a significant fire hazard.

Consumers are urged to cease using these smoke detectors immediately. For guidance on obtaining a full refund via Amazon.com, please reach out via email at lmm15957491237@163.com. Discarded smoke alarms can be disposed of in regular household trash.
recalls.Title: LShome 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms Recalled Due to Failure to Alert During Fires, Risking Serious Injury or Death

Images.Caption: Recalled LSHOME 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarm packaging
Recalled LSHOME 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from February 2024 to December 2025 for approximately $30.

Hazards.Name: Delayed alarm activation due to high sensing threshold, posing a fire risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the alarms and contact for a full refund; dispose of alarms in household trash.

recalls.metaDescription: LShome recalls 3-Pack Smoke Detector Fire Alarms due to failure to alert during fires, risking serious injury or death. Immediate action required.

recalls.Description: Apollo America is recalling its Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors, model number 51000-600, which were sold in white. These detectors may not alert users to a fire or carbon monoxide leak, leading to potential smoke inhalation, carbon monoxide poisoning, or even death. The front of the device displays a "Replace by" date between June and October 2034, while the back features the brand name and model number.
recalls.Title: Apollo America Recalls Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors Due to Alert Failure; Serious Injury or Death Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Vivint at 800-281-9667, email recalls@vivint.com, or visit https://www.vivint.com/smoke-co-recall for more details.

Products.Name: Apollo America Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Images.Caption: Recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector
Back of recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector
Recalled Apollo America Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Manufacturers.Name: Apollo America Inc., of Auburn Hills, Michigan

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.Vivint.com and through door-to-door and telephone sales by Vivint from June to October 2024, priced between $50 and $100.

Hazards.Name: Potential failure to alert users of fire or carbon monoxide leaks

Remedies.Name: Free replacement detector provided by Vivint

recalls.metaDescription: Apollo America recalls smoke and CO detectors due to alert failure, risking serious injury or death. Contact Vivint for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Three61 is recalling its Samurai-branded mini smoke alarms, model SM1, due to a serious safety issue. These compact smoke alarms, measuring approximately 2.5 inches in each dimension, were sold in sets of two or three and included lithium-ion batteries, adhesive stickers, screws, mounting plates, and an instruction manual. The front of the device features the word 'Smoke' beneath a button, while the model number can be found on the packaging.
recalls.Title: Three61 Recalls Samurai Mini Smoke Alarms for Fire Alert Failure; Sold Exclusively at HSN
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach Three61 via email at recall@samuraibrands.com or visit www.samuraibrands.com/recall for further assistance. A recall banner is also available on the homepage for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - front
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - back
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm – packaging
Recalled Samurai Mini Smoke Alarm - packaging

Injuries.Name: Three61 has received eight reports of the alarms failing to activate in the presence of smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively through HSN and online at HSN.com from July 2020 to November 2024, priced between $40 and $50.

Importers.Name: Three61 LLC, of Palm Harbor, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of smoke inhalation or death due to failure to alert consumers of a fire.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Three61 for a free replacement alarm. To initiate the process, they must mark the recalled smoke alarm with 'Recalled' using a permanent marker and submit a photo of it on the recall website. The replacement will be shipped immediately upon registration.

recalls.metaDescription: Three61 recalls Samurai Mini Smoke Alarms due to failure to alert of fires. Contact for free replacements and safety information.

recalls.Description: The recall affects CHZHVAN brand smoke and carbon monoxide detectors, model JKD-512. These detectors, made of white plastic and measuring approximately four inches in diameter, feature a black plastic circle with a digital display on the front. The model name 'CHZHVAN' is displayed above the screen, and the back includes slots for AAA batteries along with important product information and instructions.
recalls.Title: CHZHVAN Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detectors Recalled for Fire Alert Failure
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Haikouhuidishangmaoyouxiangongsi via email at l18276710356@163.com, or visit www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A4INNIJC6B92S&asin=B0C2ZCTCZ8&ref_=dp_merchant_link&isAmazonFulfilled=1 or www.haikouhuidi.com and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: CHZHVAN Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector

Images.Caption: Recalled CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector (Front with Packaging)
Recalled CHZHVAN Combination Smoke and Carbon Monoxide Detector (Back with Packaging)

Injuries.Name: There are five reports of the detectors failing to alarm in the presence of smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from August 2023 to January 2024, priced between $18 and $59.

Hazards.Name: Failure to activate, risking lack of fire alert for consumers.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled detectors immediately and contact Haikouhuidishangmaoyouxiangongsi for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for CHZHVAN smoke and carbon monoxide detectors due to fire alert failure. Contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Honeywell is recalling all L-Series Low Frequency Sounders, Sounder Strobes, and Compact Sounders due to a potential malfunction that may prevent the fire alarm system from alerting consumers in the event of a fire. These devices feature square designs with white and red plastic housings, suitable for wall mounting. The sounders and strobes measure approximately 5.6 by 4.7 inches, while the compact sounder is about 5.3 by 3.5 inches. Model numbers and date codes, including 3034 and 3035, can be found on the product label and packaging.
recalls.Title: Honeywell Recalls L-Series Low Frequency Fire Alarm Sounders and Strobes Due to Alert Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact System Sensor at 800-736-7672, Monday through Friday from 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, email LFSounder.recall@systemsensor.com, or visit https://buildings.honeywell.com/us/en/brands/our-brands/system-sensor/resources/recall or www.SystemSensor.com and select 'Recall' at the top for further details.

Products.Name: L-Series Low Frequency Sounders and Strobes

Injuries.Name: Honeywell has received two reports of low or no sound output during installation. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Honeywell International Inc., of Charlotte, North Carolina
System Sensor, of St. Charles, Illinois

Retailers.Name: Available through Honeywell authorized System Sensor installers and fire equipment distributors nationwide from March 2023 to April 2023 for approximately $107.

Hazards.Name: Potential malfunction may prevent fire alarm system from alerting consumers.

Remedies.Name: Contact the firm for a free inspection and repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Honeywell recalls L-Series fire alarm sounders and strobes due to a risk of failure to alert consumers in a fire. Learn more about the recall.
